---
name: distill-style
description: |
  Generate or refresh a `wiki/style/<repo>.md` page from the dominant human
  reviewer's PR comments. Call this when an agent is about to do non-trivial
  work in a repo whose style page is missing or stale (>90 days).

  The output is a single wiki page that distills 10–20 convictions held by
  the people who actually approve PRs in that repo, each backed by ≥1
  verbatim quote and ≥1 PR citation. The point is to make code more likely
  to land cleanly with this team.

  Triggers: "/distill-style", "no style page for this repo", "extract Amaury's
  conviction list", "what does the reviewer want", standing-instructions hint
  when current repo lacks `wiki/style/<repo>.md`.

  - Bash
  - Read
  - Grep
  - Glob
  - Edit
  - Write
  - WebFetch
---

# /distill-style — extract a repo's reviewer convictions into a wiki page

## When to invoke

The current repo has no `~/.leadbay/knowledge/wiki/style/<repo>.md`, OR the
existing page's `last_verified` is older than 90 days, AND the agent is about
to do non-trivial coding work that will be PR-reviewed.

Already covered: `leadbay/backend` (Amaury / @TheKinrar) and `leadbay/frontend`
(Damien / @damienesp). Don't regenerate those unless the user asks for a refresh.

## When NOT to invoke

- The repo is small / one-off / not maintained by humans.
- The repo has no merged human PRs (e.g. greenfield, single-author).
- The user is asking you to learn the **product**, not the **code style** —
  use `/knowledge-explore` for that.
- The wiki page already exists and `last_verified` is recent (<90 days).

## What this produces

A single file at `~/.leadbay/knowledge/wiki/style/<repo-shortname>.md` with:

```yaml
---
type: style
title: <Repo> code style — convictions of the people reviewing
aliases: [<repo>-style, <lang>-style, <reviewer-handle>-style]
backend_names:
  - <owner>/<repo>
ui_locations: []
confidence: <1-10>
last_verified: <YYYY-MM-DD>
sources:
  - .context/style-analysis/<handle>-review-comments.json
  - https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pulls?q=is%3Apr+author%3A<handle>
related: []
---
```

Body: 10–20 numbered rules, each tagged `(must)` / `(prefer)` / `(quirk)`,
each with ≥1 verbatim quote (in `>` blockquotes) and ≥1 PR citation.

## Workflow

### 1. Identify the dominant reviewer

```bash
# Detect repo from cwd
REPO_URL=$(git config --get remote.origin.url)
REPO=$(echo "$REPO_URL" | sed -E 's#\.git$##; s#^.*[:/]([^/]+/[^/]+)$#\1#')
SHORT=$(echo "$REPO" | sed 's#.*/##')

# Top reviewers by # of review comments in last 12 months, excluding bots
gh api -X GET "repos/$REPO/pulls/comments" \
  --paginate \
  -F per_page=100 \
  --jq '.[] | select(.created_at > (now - 365*86400 | todate)) | .user.login' \
  | grep -v -E '\[bot\]|claude-bot|dependabot|renovate' \
  | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5
```

Pick the top 1, occasionally top 2 if the gap is small. If there's no clear
dominant reviewer (top one has < 30 review comments in 12 months), abort —
write a `questions/` entry instead saying "no dominant reviewer; style page
not warranted".

### 2. Pull verbatim review comments

```bash
mkdir -p .context/style-analysis
HANDLE=<picked-reviewer>
gh api -X GET "repos/$REPO/pulls/comments" \
  --paginate \
  -F per_page=100 \
  --jq "[.[] | select(.user.login == \"$HANDLE\") | {body, line, path, pr: (.pull_request_url | split(\"/\") | last)}]" \
  > .context/style-analysis/$HANDLE-review-comments.json
```

Aim for ≥80 comments. If <30, the style page won't be useful — abort.

### 3. Pull their authored PRs

```bash
gh api -X GET "repos/$REPO/pulls" \
  --paginate \
  -F state=closed \
  -F per_page=100 \
  --jq "[.[] | select(.user.login == \"$HANDLE\" and .merged_at != null) | {number, title, merged_at}]" \
  > .context/style-analysis/$HANDLE-authored-prs.tsv
```

Their own PR titles often signal what they CARE about (you'll see things
like "Fix missing index", "Drop unused HNSW indexes", "Optimize ... upserts").
Cite 3–5 of these in the page — they're proof of conviction by action.

### 4. Cluster comments into rules (use an Explore subagent)

Launch an Agent with subagent_type=Explore. Brief:

> Read `.context/style-analysis/<handle>-review-comments.json` and produce
> 10–20 distinct rules the reviewer keeps making. For each rule, return:
> - one-line headline
> - tag: (must) | (prefer) | (quirk)
> - 2–4 verbatim quotes from different PRs
> - the PR numbers
> - 1-paragraph explanation in your voice
>
> Cluster aggressively — don't make 20 rules from 30 comments. Combine
> comments that say the same thing with different examples. Drop comments
> that are about a one-off bug rather than a general pattern.

### 5. Write the wiki page

Use the format of `wiki/style/backend.md` and `wiki/style/frontend.md` as
templates. Frontmatter MUST include:
- `type: style` (validated by JSON schema)
- `aliases` with `<repo>-style` AND `<reviewer-handle>-style`
- `backend_names: [<owner>/<repo>]`
- `confidence: 7-10` based on volume of evidence (7 if ~30 comments,
  10 if 100+)
- `last_verified: <today>` (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `sources` listing the JSON file AND the GitHub PR-search URL

Body must:
- Open with one paragraph naming the reviewer and their GitHub handle.
- Number the rules.
- Tag each rule (must) / (prefer) / (quirk).
- For each rule: ≥1 verbatim quote in a `>` blockquote, with PR # citation.
- Close with sections "Anti-patterns — things they'll reject" and "Workflow —
  how they expect PRs to flow".

## Hard caps

- **Max 20 rules.** Beyond that the page becomes unread.
- **Max 1 NEW page per invocation.** Always exactly one — `wiki/style/<repo>.md`.
- **Min 30 review comments OR abort.** Don't write a fluff page.
- **Verbatim quotes only.** No paraphrasing reviewer comments.

## Validation

```bash
LB_LANE=cold python3 ~/.leadbay/knowledge/bin/lb-knowledge-validate-all
```

Must pass before commit.

## Refresh mode

If the page already exists and `last_verified > 90 days ago`:

1. Read the existing page first.
2. Pull only comments newer than `last_verified` (filter on `created_at`).
3. If <10 new comments: bump `last_verified` to today and commit. The rules
   haven't moved — the reviewer hasn't said anything new worth filing.
4. If ≥10 new comments: re-run clustering on the **combined** old+new corpus.
   Add new rules, remove rules whose evidence is all stale (>2 years) and
   not reinforced by recent comments. Bump `confidence` if rules held up;
   drop it if they didn't.

## Commit + push

Same convention as `/learnings-from-pr`: write directly to
`leadbay/knowledge:main` (no PR review for knowledge edits). One commit,
message format:

```
wiki/style/<repo>: distilled <reviewer-handle>'s convictions (<N> rules)

Sourced from <comment-count> review comments + <pr-count> authored PRs.
Refresh-after: <date 90 days from now>.
```

## Failure modes to avoid

- **Don't invent rules from a single comment.** If a conviction only shows up
  once, it's noise. Two distinct comments minimum.
- **Don't paraphrase quotes.** The reviewer's exact wording is the artifact.
- **Don't grade the reviewer's taste.** Style pages capture what THEY want,
  not what's "objectively correct".
- **Don't cite the bot.** If `claude-bot` or `[bot]` appears in the author
  list, filter it out.
- **Don't write the page if comment volume is too low.** A page based on
  20 comments will be wrong half the time and worse than no page.
